Binary Embedding

Embed version information in compiled binaries at build time. This allows executables to report their version without external files.

Overview

Binary embedding injects version strings into executables during compilation. The approach varies by language:

LanguageMechanismWhen Resolved
Go-ldflags -XLink time
Rustoption_env!()Compile time
C/C++-D definesPreprocess time

Benefits

  • Self-contained: Binary knows its version without external files
  • Immutable: Version can’t be changed after build
  • Verifiable: Easy to check what version is running
  • Cacheable: CI can cache builds by version

Go Example

See Go Integration for full details.

Rust Example

See Rust Integration for full details.

C/C++ Example

See C Integration and C++ Integration for full details.

Extended Version Info

Embed multiple values for rich version information:

Version Flag Implementation
